name: Gallium Arsenide Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Gallium Arsenide. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for GaAs surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Green wavelength optimized for GaAs absorption characteristics

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning resolution

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for GaAs surface cleaning without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Number of cleaning passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning coverage

power.description: Typical range for 532 nm pulsed lasers in semiconductor cleaning to avoid thermal damage to GaAs (melting point ~1238°C, high reflectivity at green wavelengths). Starting at 20 W balances efficiency and safety; max limited to prevent substrate ablation. Source: Studies on laser cleaning of III-V semiconductors (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. 2018 on GaAs thresholds).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contamination removal without excessive heat buildup on GaAs; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster for light particles. Recommended 500 mm/s for optimal balance in industrial setups. Source: Industry manuals from IPG Photonics and Adapt Laser Systems for precision cleaning.

pulse_width.description: Picosecond regime preferred over nanoseconds (5-100 ns) for GaAs to minimize heat-affected zone due to its moderate thermal conductivity (~50 W/m·K); femtoseconds (<1 ps) possible but less common for industrial cleaning. Recommended 10 ps for precise contaminant ablation. Source: Laser physics research in Opt. Express (2020) on ultrafast cleaning of semiconductors.

repetition_rate.description: Low to moderate rates for GaAs to limit heat accumulation while enabling fast cleaning; higher rates (>20 kHz) increase speed but risk thermal damage. Recommended 10 kHz for balanced throughput. Source: Academic literature on pulsed laser cleaning (e.g., Appl. Surf. Sci. 2019, trade-offs in semiconductor applications).

energy_density.description: Fluence below GaAs damage threshold (~1.5 J/cm² at 532 nm) for safe cleaning; typical for removing contaminants like particles or oxides. Recommended 0.5 J/cm² to ablate surface layers without substrate harm. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Phys. Rev. B (2017) for GaAs at visible wavelengths.

spot_size.description: Small spots for fine detail on GaAs wafers (e.g., surface prep), larger for broad areas; 100 μm balances resolution and coverage in industrial cleaning. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf lasers for micro-cleaning applications.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy layers on GaAs to ensure complete removal without overexposure. Recommended 2 passes for uniform results in semiconductor processing. Source: Industry case studies (e.g., SPIE Proc. 2021 on laser cleaning protocols for GaAs devices).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on GaAs surfaces; 50% optimal for even cleaning without gaps or excessive overlap-induced damage. Higher for intricate patterns. Source: Process optimization in J. Laser Appl. (2016), standard for scanned laser cleaning.

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical, mechanical, chemical, and structural properties affecting cleaning outcomes and material integrity

laserDamageThreshold.citation: G. R. Huggett, F. W. Chambers, and R. A. House, Applied Physics Letters, 1975, Vol. 27, Issue 6, pp. 298-300, DOI: 10.1063/1.88493
laserDamageThreshold.context: Undoped single-crystal Gallium Arsenide (99.999% purity), room temperature (25°C), 1.06 μm wavelength, 20 ns pulse duration, single-shot measurement in vacuum

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

ablationThreshold.citation: J. Bonse, J. Krüger, S. Höhm, A. Rosenfeld, 'Femtosecond laser ablation of gallium arsenide: threshold fluences and incubation effects', Applied Surface Science, 2012, DOI: 10.1016/j.apsusc.2012.04.123
ablationThreshold.context: Undoped single-crystal GaAs (purity >99.99%), room temperature (25°C), 800 nm wavelength Ti:sapphire femtosecond laser, 100 fs pulse duration, measured in air at 1 atm
